hypothesis: If tensor completion surrogate models (CPD-S, NeAT, and their ensemble) are used to predict Young's modulus and mass-normalized Young's modulus of lattice structures, then they will achieve higher predictive accuracy (R²) than Gaussian Process, XGBoost, and MLP baselines across both uniform and biased sampling regimes—particularly at small training sizes and under non-uniform sampling—because tensor completion leverages the low‑rank multilinear structure of the design-parameter tensor to generalize better from sparse or unevenly sampled data.
